info.name: PrivacyPilot
info.description:
PrivacyPilot (GDPR/RODO) API — one folder per controller. Base port 9004.

AUTH: every /api/privacypilot/** endpoint requires the RegulaOne session. Sign in through the RegulaOne SSO login first, then copy the value of the httpOnly `idToken` cookie into the collection variable {{idToken}} (DevTools → Application → Cookies, or the login response). The backend forwards that cookie to RegulaOne GET /api/auth/me to resolve the caller; the tenant is taken from that verified session (never from the client).

RESPONSES: the standard envelope { success, message, data, errorCode, status }. Errors: 401 (no/invalid session), 403 (role not permitted), 404 (not found / other tenant), 400 (validation).

variable.description: RegulaOne session cookie value (see collection description).
Id of a processing activity, for the by-id requests.

item.name: Processing Activities (ROPA)
item.description: Record of Processing Activities — Art. 30 GDPR. Controller (Art. 30(1)) and processor (Art. 30(2)) entries. The DPIA verdict is computed server-side from the screening criteria.

request.description: All live (non-archived) activities for the caller's tenant, newest change first. Roles: Admin, Compliance Officer, DPO, Auditor.
One activity, only if it belongs to the caller's tenant (else 404). Roles: Admin, Compliance Officer, DPO, Auditor.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "HR & payroll",
  "department": "hr",
  "role": "controller",
  "purpose": "Maintaining personnel files and running payroll.",
  "lawfulBasis": "legal_obligation",
  "provisionStatement": "Required by the Labour Code; without this data we cannot run employment.",
  "dataSubjects": ["employees"],
  "dataCategories": ["identity", "contact", "financial", "employment"],
  "art9Condition": "b",
  "art10": false,
  "dataSources": ["Directly from employees", "Social insurance (ZUS)"],
  "recipients": ["public_authorities", "banks", "payroll_bureau"],
  "vendorIds": [],
  "transfer": false,
  "transferIds": [],
  "retentionPeriod": "10 years after employment ends",
  "retentionBasis": "Art. 94(9b) of the Labour Code",
  "toms": ["tom_encryption_rest", "tom_access_control", "tom_mfa", "tom_backups"],
  "dpiaCriteria": ["special_categories"]
}

request.description: Create a new activity (starts as DRAFT). Server sets tenant, owner and status; the DPIA verdict is derived from dpiaCriteria (>=2 -> required, 1 -> recommended, 0 -> not_indicated). Roles: Admin, Compliance Officer.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "HR & payroll",
  "department": "hr",
  "role": "controller",
  "purpose": "Maintaining personnel files and running payroll.",
  "lawfulBasis": "legal_obligation",
  "provisionStatement": "Required by the Labour Code.",
  "dataSubjects": ["employees"],
  "dataCategories": ["identity", "contact", "financial", "employment"],
  "art9Condition": "b",
  "art10": false,
  "dataSources": ["Directly from employees"],
  "recipients": ["public_authorities", "banks", "payroll_bureau"],
  "vendorIds": [],
  "transfer": false,
  "transferIds": [],
  "retentionPeriod": "50 years (employee records created before 2019)",
  "retentionBasis": "Art. 94(9b) of the Labour Code",
  "toms": ["tom_encryption_rest", "tom_access_control", "tom_mfa", "tom_backups"],
  "dpiaCriteria": ["special_categories", "large_scale"]
}

request.description: Replace the editable fields of an existing activity. Only the fields that changed are recorded in the audit trail. Here dpiaCriteria has 2 entries, so the verdict recomputes to 'required'. Roles: Admin, Compliance Officer.
Sign off an activity (status -> approved). Roles: Admin, DPO.
Soft-delete (archive) an activity: hidden from the register but kept on disk for the 10-year retention rule, with an audit entry. Roles: Admin.
